import pytest

from your_module import count_vowels


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "input_str, expected",
    [
        ("", 0),                          # empty string
        ("bcdfg", 0),                     # no vowels
        ("a", 1),                         # single vowel
        ("A", 1),                         # uppercase vowel
        ("hello", 2),                    # normal case
        ("HELLO", 2),                    # uppercase word
        ("The quick brown fox", 5),      # mixed case with spaces
        ("aeiou", 5),                   # all vowels
        ("AEIOU", 5),                   # all uppercase vowels
        ("Python", 1),                 # one vowel
        ("12345", 0),                    # digits
        ("h3ll0", 0),                    # alphanumeric, no vowels
        ("rhythm", 0),                   # vowel-less word
        ("Beautiful", 5),              # mixed case with multiple vowels
    ],
)
def test_count_vowels_happy_and_edge_cases(input_str, expected):
    assert count_vowels(input_str) == expected


def test_count_vowels_unicode_non_vowels():
    # Characters outside ASCII vowels should not be counted
    assert count_vowels("éïöü") == 0


def test_count_vowels_with_punctuation():
    assert count_vowels("hello, world!") == 3


def test_count_vowels_non_string_raises_attribute_error():
    # The function expects a string; non-strings without .lower() should fail
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
        count_vowels(123)


def test_count_vowels_none_raises_attribute_error():
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
        count_vowels(None)
